text:
Here’s your 6-Week “Mind & Body Reset” Coaching Framework — a condensed, high-impact version of your 12-week signature system, preserving every transformational element but focusing on the fastest, most emotionally resonant results for women 40–60 who’ve lost hope and want to feel calm, confident, and in control again.

This version integrates:

🌿 Jody Moore’s Feel Great System → mindset, self-coaching, emotional regulation, integrity


💫 Mindy Pelz’s hormone-friendly rhythm → gentle fasting awareness, nourishment, and rest cycles


🩷 Your “Better Than Perfect” coaching voice → warm, encouraging, compassionate, but firm




🌿 6-Week Mind & Body Reset Framework

Theme: “Reclaim Your Body. Rewire Your Mind. Reset Your Life.”

A 6-week journey to peace with food, confidence in your body, and power in your thoughts.



🩷 Week 1 – Awareness & Intention: Begin with Mindfulness

Core Focus: Understand your relationship with food, body, and emotion.
 Coaching Goal: Shift from autopilot to awareness.

Mindset: Notice your thoughts. No judgment, just curiosity.


C-T-F-A-R: What am I thinking about my body? How does that make me feel?


Habits:


Start daily Unimate + Balance routine (FGS)


Hydration goal: ½ your body weight (oz)


Journal daily for 5 minutes


Mantra: “Awareness is the first step toward peace.”


Homework: Write your “why” — what do you want to feel by the end of 6 weeks?




🌸 Week 2 – Decision & Commitment: Build Self-Trust

Core Focus: Learn how to honor your plan — not perfectly, but consistently.
 Coaching Goal: Create safety and predictability through small wins.

Mindset: Make decisions from love, not guilt.


C-T-F-A-R: What happens when I think “I blew it”? What could I think instead?


Habits:


Daily food rhythm (3 intentional meals or FGS rhythm)


Pre-plan your day each morning


Keep one small promise daily (walk, water, or bedtime)


Mantra: “Decide once. Honor daily.”


Homework: Reflect nightly — Did I honor my plan? Why or why not?




🌼 Week 3 – Emotional Mastery: Feel Instead of Fix

Core Focus: End emotional eating and all-or-nothing thinking.
 Coaching Goal: Build emotional literacy and learn to process, not resist, feelings.

Mindset: No emotion is dangerous when allowed.


C-T-F-A-R: When I feel anxious, what am I thinking?


Habits:


3 deep breaths before eating


Pause when you feel an urge — name the emotion


Write 1 thought daily that calms your nervous system


Mantra: “You can handle an urge without obeying it.”


Homework: Track emotional triggers — what feelings most often lead to buffering?




🌻 Week 4 – Body Respect & Consistency

Core Focus: Rebuild trust with your body through calm, consistent actions.
 Coaching Goal: Move from control to connection.

Mindset: “My body isn’t broken — it’s communicating.”


C-T-F-A-R: What do I make it mean when the scale fluctuates?


Habits:


Continue FGS routine


Add gentle movement (walking, stretching, yoga)


Prioritize rest and bedtime consistency


Mantra: “Progress, not perfection.”


Homework: Journal: What signs show my body is healing or balancing?




🌷 Week 5 – Identity & Empowerment

Core Focus: Step into the woman who lives calm, confident, and free.
 Coaching Goal: Anchor new beliefs and habits into your identity.

Mindset: Be → Do → Have. Act like the woman you’re becoming.


C-T-F-A-R: What would my future self think, feel, and do today?


Habits:


Re-affirm daily “I am” statements


Morning visualization: 2 minutes of gratitude and self-belief


Continue honoring one micro-habit per day


Mantra: “I am her already.”


Homework: Write a letter from your future self thanking you for showing up now.




🌺 Week 6 – Integration & Celebration: Better Than Perfect

Core Focus: Maintain balance, celebrate growth, and design your next 90 days.
 Coaching Goal: Solidify lifelong confidence and self-coaching tools.

Mindset: You don’t need perfection — you need peace.


C-T-F-A-R: How do I want to think about myself going forward?


Habits:


Choose your maintenance rhythm (FGS, journaling, movement)


Celebrate weekly wins — emotional, physical, or mindset


Teach one concept you’ve learned to someone else (anchors it deeper)


Mantra: “This is not the end — it’s my new normal.”


Homework: Reflect: What belief changed my life the most?




✨ Program Promise

In just six weeks, women will:

Rebuild trust with their body and mind.


Experience stable energy and emotional calm.


Learn how to self-coach through cravings, shame, or fear.


Create a sustainable rhythm with food, rest, and thought management.


Feel peaceful, confident, and steady — not perfect, but Better Than Perfect.

PROGRAM: Reclaim Your Body. Rewire Your Mind. Reset Your Life.

A 6-week coaching experience for women 40–60 who want to finally lose weight, keep it off, and quiet the constant food chatter.



WEEK 1: Reset Your Nervous System and Quiet the Food Noise

Focus: Awareness, emotional regulation, and calming the brain
 You’ll help them:
 • Understand why nothing has worked (it’s not their fault)
 • Learn the Feel Great foundations: awareness, emotional regulation
 • Interrupt the “I can’t trust myself around food” story
 • Use simple Better Than Perfect tools to reduce urgency, guilt, and overeating
 • Begin light metabolic priming: morning hydration, protein-first structure, circadian rhythm reset

Outcome: They feel calmer. Food noise starts to quiet. They feel hope again.



WEEK 2: Stabilize Your Hormones and Turn Hunger Off

Focus: Menopause/perimenopause hormone repair
 You’ll help them:
 • Learn the hormonal rules for women 40–60
 • Balance insulin, cortisol, and leptin for fewer cravings
 • Introduce Dr. Mindy Pelz’s beginner fasting windows (gentle 12–13 hours)
 • Teach how to use food timing to reduce snacking and emotional cravings
 • Move from reactive eating to intentional eating

Outcome: Hunger stabilizes. Cravings decrease. They experience their first quiet afternoon.



WEEK 3: Rewire Your Thoughts. Rebuild Trust With Your Body.

Focus: Belief work and self-coaching
 You’ll help them:
 • Identify the thoughts causing sabotage
 • Build new core beliefs: “I can trust myself around food”
 • Create their personal self-coaching script using Jody Moore’s model
 • Replace all-or-nothing habits with Better Than Perfect consistency
 • Introduce metabolic flexibility protocols

Outcome: They feel in control again. They stop the guilt spiral and start feeling proud.



WEEK 4: Activate Fat Burning With Fasting + Food Freedom Structure

Focus: Metabolic repair and deeper fasting
 You’ll help them:
 • Progress to 14–16 hour fasts (based on readiness)
 • Implement hormone-friendly meal rhythms for perimenopause/menopause
 • Learn fat-burning food pairings (protein + smart carbs + healthy fats)
 • Create their personal “Food Freedom Framework”
 • Break emotional eating loops using emotional regulation and self-coaching

Outcome: Weight starts shifting. Bloating goes down. They feel lighter in body and mind.



WEEK 5: Master Emotional Regulation and Stress-Driven Eating

Focus: Nervous system healing + emotional eating
 You’ll help them:
 • Understand the emotional origin of overeating
 • Use Feel Great tools to process urge, stress, boredom, anxiety
 • Create new responses to triggers
 • Practice self-compassion and Better Than Perfect food choices
 • Learn metabolic repair for PMS, perimenopause, and cortisol spikes

Outcome: They stop turning to food when stressed. They feel calm, steady, and in control.



WEEK 6: Integrate Your New Identity and Make the Weight Loss Stick

Focus: Maintenance, identity shift, long-term success
 You’ll help them:
 • Step into the identity of a woman who trusts herself around food
 • Build a forever plan that doesn’t rely on willpower
 • Create hormone-friendly routines for long-term health
 • Practice advanced self-coaching for thoughts about weight, food, and aging
 • Celebrate wins and build their “Reset for Life” maintenance strategy

Outcome: They feel confident. Food noise is quiet. Weight loss feels doable and sustainable.

THE RECLAIM FRAMEWORK

Reclaim Your Body. Rewire Your Mind. Reset Your Life.

R
 Regulate Your Nervous System
 (Week 1)

E
 Ease Food Noise and Emotional Urges
 (Week 1)

C
 Calm Your Hormones and Cravings
 (Week 2)

L
 Learn to Trust Your Body Again
 (Week 3)

A
 Activate Fat Burning and Metabolic Healing
 (Week 4)

I
 Interpret Your Emotions Instead of Eating Them
 (Week 5)

M
 Make Your New Identity the Default
 (Week 6)



HOW RECLAIM FITS INTO YOUR 6-WEEK PROGRAM

**WEEK 1

R + E: Regulate Your Nervous System + Ease Food Noise**
 Your women start by getting calm.
 They learn emotional regulation, reduce urgency around food, quiet the chatter, and understand why they’ve felt out of control.
 This sets the foundation for the entire program.

**WEEK 2

C: Calm Your Hormones and Cravings**
 Hormone stabilization begins.
 They learn insulin, cortisol, and leptin basics for women 40–60.
 Gentle fasting opens the door to hunger control and fewer cravings.

**WEEK 3

L: Learn to Trust Your Body Again**
 They rewrite the “I can’t trust myself around food” identity.
 You guide them through belief shifts and self-coaching so they feel capable, steady, and proud.

**WEEK 4

A: Activate Fat Burning and Metabolic Healing**
 This is where real change compounds.
 Fasting protocols deepen, metabolic flexibility increases, and they feel lighter physically and mentally.

**WEEK 5

I: Interpret Your Emotions Instead of Eating Them**
 Stress eating, boredom eating, overwhelm eating — all addressed.
 They stop fearing urges and learn emotional mastery.

**WEEK 6

M: Make Your New Identity the Default**
 You lock in the new lifestyle, the new thoughts, and the new patterns.
 They walk away feeling: calm, confident, and actually in control of food.



THE RECLAIM FRAMEWORK (summarized clearly)

R Regulate Your Nervous System
 E Ease Food Noise
 C Calm Hormones and Cravings
 L Learn to Trust Your Body
 A Activate Fat Burning
 I Interpret Emotions
 M Make It Your Identity

Weekly 50-minute coaching sessions
 Text and voice message support with clear boundaries
 6 weeks, 1:1, premium, intimate, high-touch but not draining

Now I’ll build the full 1:1 RECLAIM Coaching Container in clean, simple language so it feels clear to you and attractive to your ideal client.



THE 1:1 RECLAIM COACHING CONTAINER

Reclaim Your Body. Rewire Your Mind. Reset Your Life.

Length: 6 weeks
 Format: Private 1:1 coaching
 Calls: One 50-minute coaching session each week
 Support: Text + voice message support Monday–Thursday during set hours
 Resources: Simple tools, fasting guides, mindset prompts, weekly check-ins
 Delivery: Online (Zoom + private chat access)



WHAT YOUR CLIENT ACTUALLY GETS

1. Weekly 1:1 Coaching Sessions (50 minutes)

Every session follows your RECLAIM framework:
 • regulate
 • ease
 • calm
 • learn
 • activate
 • interpret
 • make-it-your-identity

This is where the real mind rewiring and emotional regulation happens.

2. Text + Voice Messaging Support (with boundaries)

You stay supportive, grounded, and present without burning out.
 Clear boundaries might look like:
 • Monday–Thursday
 • Responses within 24 hours
 • Quick coaching, emotional support, and course corrections
 • No weekends, no emergencies

Women love this. It feels safe, personal, and doable.

3. Your RECLAIM Weekly Planner + Check-ins

They get:
 • Weekly focus
 • Fasting window guide
 • Hormone-friendly eating prompts
 • Mindset questions using Jody Moore’s model
 • Better Than Perfect action steps
 • Accountability checkmarks that work for busy women

4. Your Resource Library

Keep it simple:
 • Fasting basics
 • What to eat in each hormone phase
 • How to break a fast
 • How to shut down food noise
 • Quick self-coaching scripts
 • Emotional regulation exercises

This is the “home base” that helps them stay consistent.



HOW THE 6 WEEKS FLOW IN THE CONTAINER

WEEK 1: Regulate + Ease

Calm the nervous system. Reduce food noise. Start metabolic priming.

WEEK 2: Calm Hormones

Gentle fasting windows, insulin + cortisol reset.

WEEK 3: Learn Trust Again

Rewrite self-doubt. End the “I can’t control food” story.

WEEK 4: Activate Fat Burning

Fasting progression, metabolic flexibility, lighter body.

WEEK 5: Interpret Emotions

Stop stress eating. Emotional regulation made simple.

WEEK 6: Make It Your Identity

Lock in habits. Build your Reset For Life plan.



WHAT MAKES THIS CONTAINER PREMIUM AND SELLABLE

This is not a generic health program.
 It’s a high-touch transformation for women who have tried everything and feel broken.

You’re giving them:

• private support
 • real-time guidance
 • hormone expertise
 • mindset rewiring
 • emotional regulation
 • Better Than Perfect consistency
 • and a path that finally makes weight loss feel doable

Women 40–60 will pay for safety, clarity, privacy, and results.

It’s not because your weak. (Food cravings) Frame work

Here is the information from the video to turn into, A concise summary, key, coaching lessons and client friendly framework, coaching questions for eating urges. Thought work examples (CTFAR style) practical exercises. Action steps for your Mind and Body Reset clients. Neuroscience content by Nora: Here is what actually happens in your brain when you feel the urge to comfort eat. Your brain is not hungry. It detected an uncomfortable emotional state and searched its memory for the fastest available source of dopamine. Food won. Not because you are weak. Because it has the best track record what actually happens in your brain when you comfort eat.Here is what actually happens in your brain when you feel the urge to comfort eat. Understanding that mechanism will help you relate to that urge every time it appears. The moment you experience, stress, loneliness, boredom or emotional discomfort your brain does something automatic and completely predictable. It searches its memory for the fastest available source of dopamine for most people the search returns food. Specifically high sugar, high fat, high calorie food. And the reason is neurological not moral. Your brain learned through thousands of repititions eating those foods produces a rapid dopamine release in your nucleus accumbens. It is not hungry. It is seeking neurochemical relief from an uncomfortable emotional state. This is why comfort eating almost always involves specific foods rather that whatever is available. Your brain is not craving nutrition. It is craving a dopamine source it has a prove track record with, but here is what makes the urge so persistent and hard to reason with. The discomfort driving the urge is real. Your brain genuinely detected some thing uncomfortable, loneliness, stress, emptiness, boredom. And it is trying to solve it with the fastest tool it knows. The problem is not the solution your brain chose.The problem is the misidentification of what the discomfort actually needs. Food delivers dopamine, but lonliness needs connection, stress needs discharge, emptiness needs meaning. Boredom needs stimulation. And dopamine from food does not address any of those underlying needs, which is why the urge returns so quickly after eating. You are not weak for comfort eating.You are a brain that learned a fast imprecise solution to a real emotional problem. The urge is information, it is telling you something is uncomfortable. The work is learning to ask what it actually needs, rather than reaching for the fastest answer your brain already knows.

This is excellent content because it removes shame while increasing awareness. It fits perfectly with your Reclaim • Rewire • Reset framework and aligns beautifully with the work you do around emotional eating, food noise, and post-GLP-1 confidence.

The Big Idea

Your urge is not the problem.
 Your urge is information.

Most women believe:

"I want cookies. I have no willpower."

What is actually happening:

"My brain detected discomfort and suggested food because food has been a reliable source of dopamine."

That shift alone changes everything.



Concise Summary

When we experience stress, loneliness, boredom, overwhelm, frustration, or emotional discomfort, the brain automatically searches for the fastest source of relief.

For many people, food has become the brain's most reliable dopamine source.

The craving isn't a sign of hunger.

It's the brain attempting to solve an emotional problem with a solution that has worked in the past.

The challenge is that food provides temporary dopamine, but it doesn't address the underlying need.

Loneliness needs connection.

Stress needs release.

Boredom needs stimulation.

Emptiness needs meaning.

The urge itself isn't bad.

The urge is information pointing toward a need that hasn't yet been addressed.



Key Coaching Lessons

Lesson 1: Stop Moralizing the Urge

Most clients make the urge mean:

I'm weak.

I'm broken.

I have no discipline.

Instead teach:

"My brain is doing exactly what brains do."

This immediately reduces shame.



Lesson 2: Separate Hunger From Relief Seeking

Ask:

"Is my body asking for food or is my brain asking for relief?"

Those are two very different needs.



Lesson 3: The Brain Loves Efficiency

The brain is not asking:

"What is the healthiest solution?"

The brain is asking:

"What is the fastest solution?"

Food often wins because it has a long history of delivering immediate dopamine.



Lesson 4: Every Urge Contains Information

Instead of fighting urges, get curious.

Ask:

"What problem is my brain trying to solve right now?"



Lesson 5: Replace the Solution, Not the Need

Many women try to eliminate the need.

The goal is not:

Stop feeling lonely.

The goal is:

Meet loneliness with connection instead of cookies.



Mind & Body Reset Framework

The REAL Method

R = Recognize

Pause.

Name the urge.

"I'm having an urge to eat."

Not:

"I need food."



E = Examine

Ask:

"What am I actually feeling?"

Possible answers:

Stress

Loneliness

Frustration

Boredom

Anxiety

Exhaustion



A = Address the Need

What would actually help?

If stress → movement

If lonely → connection

If bored → stimulation

If tired → rest

If overwhelmed → simplify



L = Let the Urge Ride

Urges are like waves.

They rise.
 They peak.
 They fall.

You do not have to obey every urge.



Coaching Questions for Eating Urges

When a client says:

"I just wanted the chips."

Ask:

Awareness Questions

What happened right before the urge?

What emotion was present?

What were you hoping food would do for you?



Curiosity Questions

What problem was your brain trying to solve?

What did you need in that moment?

What relief were you looking for?



Rewiring Questions

If food wasn't available, what would you do?

What would actually help this feeling?

What is one healthier source of relief?



Identity Questions

What would the future version of you do here?

What would a woman who trusts herself do next?

What decision would make you proud tomorrow?

Practical Exercise #1

The Need Behind the Craving

Create a journal page with:

What am I craving?



What emotion is present?



What does this emotion actually need?



What would help more than food right now?



What will Future Me thank me for?



Practical Exercise #2

Urge Surfing

When an urge appears:

Set a timer for 10 minutes.

Notice:

Where do I feel it?

Is it growing?

Is it shrinking?

What thoughts are fueling it?

Teach clients:

Urges feel permanent.

They are temporary.



Practical Exercise #3

Dopamine Menu

Create a list of non-food dopamine options.

Quick Relief

Walk outside

Dance to one song

Stretch

Prayer

Deep breathing

Connection

Text a friend

Call family

Join a group

Meaning

Journal

Read scripture

Work on a goal

Gratitude list

Stimulation

Puzzle

Audiobook

Creative project

The goal is not to remove dopamine.

The goal is to expand the brain's list of where dopamine can come from.



Action Steps for Mind & Body Reset Clients

This Week's Challenge

Before eating for a craving, pause and ask:

1. What am I feeling?
 2. What does this feeling actually need?
 3. Will food solve this problem or temporarily distract me from it?

Remember:

The urge is not an emergency.

The urge is information.

And one of the most powerful reframes you can teach your clients:

"My brain isn't asking for food.

My brain is asking for relief.

My job is to figure out what kind of relief it actually needs."
